title: Chapter 30
text: Chapter Twenty- Seven R onin recorded the passage of two hours, fifty-one minutes, and seventeen seconds before Newton slowed and said they were on the edge of the base. Not for the first time since meeting Lara, Ronin doubted if his internal clock was correct. Days, weeks, years had gone by as he ran, clutching Lara to him with as much delicacy and stability as he could simultaneously manage . She didn’t wake up . Despite his care, his pace — coupled with the uneven terrain — had jarred her several times, but she never stirred. It had been a struggle not to check her pulse and breathing every few dozen yards, even knowing that the slightest delay could doom her . Talkative as Newton had been in the shelter, he was silent during the trek . The wind rustled the brown, rough grass surrounding them as Newton stopped. The gentle, lazy hills were just high enough to block Cheyenne from view, but the city was so near that Ronin doubted the decision to bring her here . “Don’t leave me yet,” Ronin said to her softly. “I need you to remind me, every day.” How could he possibly carry on, taunted by his own memories of his time with her? Had she given even the smallest indication that she’d be all right — a flutter of her eyelids, a twitch of her fingers, a groan of pain — his frantic thought chains might have eased . Newton lifted an arm, extending a finger to indicate something in the field ahead. “ There .” Ronin’s optics scanned the area, seeking the entrance. He detected nothing but waving grass and small clouds of dust kicked up by the wind. On the other side of the hill lay the ruins of the base. He’d only passed through once since coming to Cheyenne, and it had supported Newton’s story — a cracked tarmac, overgrown with weeds, scorched black around the crumbled foundations of long-fallen buildings . Newton resumed his walk, and Ronin followed without comment. His experiences, as of late, had not instilled him with much trust in man or bot, but there was no other choice. Lara’s life depended on there being people here. People willing to help . They moved down a gradual slope, and partway up the next, where Newton paused to check their surroundings. Then he led Ronin into a natural depression that blocked the landscape from sight. He bent forward, brushing aside tufts of long, dry grass to reveal a metal hatch. Between the angle of the hill and the tough vegetation, it was extremely unlikely to be spotted . Newton gripped the handle and pulled the hatch open. It was surprisingly quiet, despite its apparent age . “This is the most direct route inside,” Newton said, looking up at Ronin, “without going several miles out of our way. We’ll have to take care getting her down .” Ronin stepped forward, glancing into the opening. Metal ladder rungs, set in a concrete wall, descended ten feet into a dark corridor. “They’re down there ?” “As I said, it’s been a long while since my last visit. I don’t imagine they’d have gone anywhere, but it’s entirely possible they relocated or perished in the time since .” Lara didn’t have time for Ronin to weight the options, didn’t have time for hesitation. If there was no help here, there was no help to be found at all . “Hold her. I’ll go down, and you can pass her to me,” Ronin said, turning to Newton. He ignored the blaring alarms from the portions of his coding that had become irrational with fear. If Newton meant them harm, what was to be gained by reactivating Ronin and leading him here? Why bother powering him back on if he had malicious intentions ? He gently passed Lara’s limp form into Newton’s outstretched arms. His limbs locked as he pulled away. She was so fragile. Even losing a few seconds of contact, when they were potentially so limited a commodity, made him doubt if this was the right thing to do . Holding her now will not help her recover . Finally, he withdrew his hands, quickly turning to the hatch. He lowered himself onto the ladder without further hesitation. The sound of his boots on the rungs echoed through the corridor, which stretched into total darkness in either direction . Ronin turned his attention upward the instant his feet were on the bottom . Newton stood over the opening, maneuvering slowly to guide Lara’s legs through. He dropped into a crouch as he lowered her, adjusting his hold so his arms were beneath her armpits. Despite the pain she should have felt at such handling, she remained unconscious, head lolling . Reaching up, Ronin placed his hands on her hips and accepted her weight, drawing her against him with his cheek pressed to her bruised midsection. He was as gentle as possible; her body had been so battered, it seemed there wasn’t an inch of her without injury . “I have her .” Newton released Lara. Ronin shifted as her torso sagged, preventing her weight from coming down on her damaged ribs or broken arm. Adjusting his hold, he cradled her against his chest, smoothing her hair out of her face . Bare metal feet clanked onto the ladder. Newton closed the latch as quietly as it had been opened, plunging the tunnel into darkness . Ronin’s optics switched to night-vision. The walls and floor were concrete, the latter coated in a fine layer of dust. Were those brush strokes? There were no footprints or other signs of habitation, but the dust didn’t appear to have settled naturally . “This way,” Newton said, voice low . They proceeded down the corridor, Newton slightly ahead, footfalls scraping lightly over the dirt-blanketed floor. There were light fixtures high on the walls every fifteen feet, but no bulbs within their wire coverings. The hints of paint on the walls were too faded to decipher . Each step heightened Ronin’s anxiety. He was carrying his beloved down a pitch-black corridor with nowhere to run, no weapons, no cover. If there was a trap waiting ahead, it would be unforgivingly effective. An increasingly strong thought-chain posited there was nothing here to help her. There was nothing in all the Dust that could help her. This place would be Lara’s tomb . Newton slowed, glancing over his shoulder. “Nearly there. Just around here .” The corridor continued ahead, farther than Ronin’s optics could see in the dark, but Newton turned into an opening just ahead. Ronin followed close behind . His vision blazed white as he was hit by a bright light. Twisting away from it, he shielded Lara’s body with his . A trap, after all . “Don’t move!” someone shouted . “Quite reassuring to see security has not grown lax,” Newton said from beside Ronin . “Are you two bots , too ?” “I am,” replied Ronin, turning his head toward the man. With his night vision switched off, humanoid silhouettes were clear against the bright spotlight, all gathered behind a waist-high barrier with firearms pointed at Ronin, Newton, and Lara. “The woman is human .” “What’s wrong with her ?” “She was beaten near to death by the self-titled Warlord of Cheyenne,” Newton said . The people behind the barricade whispered to each other for several moments . “Did he follow you ?” “No.” Ronin paused, looking down to Lara. “As far as he knows, he ended us. Left us for dead .” The spotlight shifted, and the man who’d been speaking turned to two of the other figures. “Secure the perimeter .” The pair hurried past Ronin — soldiers, dressed in matching uniforms — and down the perpendicular corridor . “If you have any weapons,” the leader said, “place them on the ground . Now .” “We’re unarmed,” Ronin replied, “and we’re out of time. If you can’t help her, tell me now, so I can find someone who can before it’s too late .” “Turn around. Slowly .” It wasn’t a request, and that made it no easier to comply. Exposing Lara to potential hostiles went against Ronin’s driving need to protect her. All the same, disobeying was likely to accelerate their mutual ends . He turned, bringing the barricade into full view. His optics blurred and focused repeatedly, battling to balance the contrast between the overwhelming light and the thick darkness behind it . “Garrison, Walker, take the girl to the infirmary,” the leader commanded . Two figures advanced, features clarifying as they neared. Both held automatic rifles, similar to the one Ronin had carried. Their uniforms matched the soldiers who went into the corridor — much repaired, camouflage faded, but well-kept nonetheless. The men slung their rifles over their shoulders . “I’ll carry her.” Ronin stepped back . The soldiers paused, hands drifting toward their weapons . “You’re in no position to make demands,” the leader said, and he, too, stepped into the light. He was a middle-aged man with short-cropped hair, double bars displayed at the center of his chest. A captain. The shred of information was from Ronin’s old life. A tiny bit of data reclaimed after so many decades . For nine seconds, Ronin and the Captain stared at one another, neither moving. Walker and Garrison stood uncertainly . “They won’t hurt her,” Newton said . “How do I know?” Ronin demanded. “How do I know they’ll take care of her ?” “We have the facilities to help her,” the Captain said, “but you’re unknowns to us. Which means we have to err on the side of caution until we can determine if you’re a threat or not .” “I can’t leave her. I won’t.” Ronin was the one wasting time now. What choice did he have, here? She’d die if he did nothing . “My name’s Cooper. Captain Edward Cooper. You have my word that she will receive the best care we can offer. The quicker you cooperate, the quicker we can take care of her .” An electric crackle skittered across Ronin’s cheek. He nodded, once, and extended his arms. Walker and Garrison stepped forward and carefully collected Lara . It’s the only chance she has , he told himself, unable to shift his optics off her until she was carried out of his vision . “Captain Cooper,” Newton said, “I can assure you that I will vouch for — ” The Captain raised a fist, silencing Newton. “You’re unknowns .” A door — heavy steel, by the sound — opened somewhere behind the spotlight. More boots marched over the concrete floor, and soon six more armed soldiers stood in the corridor, surrounding Ronin and Newton . “These gentlemen will escort you inside. You will keep your hands to yourselves. They will not answer questions, so don’t bother asking. Any sudden movements that can be interpreted as hostile will be treated as such .” The soldiers took position — four behind and two ahead, all slightly to either side . “It may be of interest to you, especially if you’re considering doing any of these gentlemen harm, that an undisclosed number of them are bots. Just as physically capable as both of you, and they have rifles. I trust you’ll behave.” Cooper stepped aside, waving the escort forward . Ronin followed the soldiers in front of him automatically, his processors dominated by Lara. Where was she now? Would she be all right? He couldn’t begin to guess at the size of this facility, couldn’t be certain he’d find her on his own . They marched through the doorway, steel door slamming shut behind them. The corridor narrowed beneath a low, arched ceiling, but the lights were functioning and the floor was free of dirt. Bundles of pipe and conduit ran along the walls . Ronin couldn’t dismiss the possibility that he’d never see Lara again. That all the time he’d spent with her was all he’d ever have; those moments would be the only ones they’d ever share. Either one of them — or both — might die before seeing the other . There’d been no goodbye. No parting words of love. Only the pain on her face, the agony of her cries. Why was that last, terrible event so much more powerful than everything else? Why did it overshadow all the rest of their time together ? The corridor hit an intersection, and Ronin turned left with the group, storing step counts and estimated measurements in his memory. They were closer to the ruined base on the surface , now . “Where is she?” he asked . “You guys actually walk around out there?” one of the soldiers asked, as though he hadn’t heard Ronin’s question. “Like, through the storms and everything ?” “Doesn’t affect bots the same,” another answered. There was something about his posture, the way he walked, that wasn’t entirely human. The pointed stripes on his chest meant he was a sergeant . “That is correct,” Newton said, “but it does have a number of averse — ” “I just ain’t been off the base in a long time,” the first soldier interrupted . “You’ve never been of the base, Ramirez,” the Sergeant said, and the group fell silent . Their boots thumped over the floor, undercut by the clacking of Newton’s bare metal feet, and their equipment harnesses jingled softly. The lights hummed, barely audible beneath the other noise. None of it could distract Ronin from thoughts of Lara. His processors tumbled through hundreds of thousands of possibilities . “Where is she?” he asked again . “ Who ?” “The woman I brought here .” “Oh, the one that looked like pounded meat?” Ramirez asked. “You do that ?” Anger flared through Ronin’s systems, catching him off-guard. To hear Lara referred to in that way — so close to Warlord’s meatbag — for someone to suggest Ronin might have had something to do with, as though he would ever harm her … Newton gripped Ronin’s forearm, squeezing. Ronin dropped his gaze. They were here to save her. He had to keep that notion at the front of his mind . “Shut the fuck up, Ramirez,” three of the other soldiers said simultaneously . “Don’t talk about her that way again,” Ronin said, and pressed his lips together . “He didn’t mean anything by it,” the Sergeant assured. “Kid just doesn’t know how to think without speaking yet. Doc’s going to do all she can to fix your woman .” Before Ronin could respond, they came to another large, metal door. One of the leading soldiers stepped forward to open it. A wave of sound spilled out, dozens of voices and activities in a jumbled mess. He was ushered through, into a huge chamber filled with people going about a variety of tasks, many of them engaged in conversation as they worked . His optics swept over the space as he walked with the soldiers, who followed the set of colored lines painted on the floor. Most of the people were human, but many bots were interspersed in the crowd — not just synths, but older models similar to Greene. Everyone was well-groomed and clean, dressed in cared-for clothing. Ronin couldn’t even identify all the work they were doing, didn’t have names or functions associated with many of their tools. He’d never seen anything of the sort during his travels . Overhead, walkways hung from the high ceiling. Several more uniformed soldiers stood upon them. They stared at Ronin and Newton as they walked past . There were several doors along the walls, and two large corridors leading out of the chamber — Armory, Barracks, Stockade in red letters over one, Laboratory, Administration, Infirmary in green over the other . Was that where Lara was, the infirmary? It was his only potential lead. The green line on the floor broke away from the others, turning down that corridor. The soldiers continued past it, along the red line. Beneath the sign that said Stockade . The corridor narrowed, and they proceeded through a series of turns, past more doors and hallways, ever deeper into the facility. Finally, they passed through an open door into a large room lined on one side with small cells . Newton and Ronin were guided into a side-chamber, a long room with a long table. There were people seated at the table, some in plain clothing, some in uniforms. Whatever conversation they’d been involved in stopped . “Have a seat,” said the man at the head of the table, lifting a hand to indicate the open chairs in front of Ronin and Newton. The insignia on his blue jacket marked him as a colonel . Ronin sat as the soldiers took up positions around the perimeter of the room. Seconds of relative silence ticked by. The room’s temperature was gradually climbing, and at least one human wore a sheen of sweat on his face . “I’m Colonel Jack Rodriguez,” the man in blue finally said, “Head of Security in this facility. Given your open association with Warlord, and the fact that you came in through an entrance that hasn’t been discovered in two hundred years, I’m very concerned about the safety of my people .” “The woman we brought, in,” Ronin said, optics focused on Rodriguez, “what is her status ?” “She’s in critical condition. Doctor Cooper and her staff are doing all they can to stabilize her .” “I want to see her .” “That’s not how this works. You represent a potential threat. Until I’m certain that threat is nonexistent , I — ” “Warlord is the one who beat her and left me deactivated. We’re unarmed, and didn’t resist when your men took us into custody. We knew where the entrance was because my companion has used it in the past .” All eyes swung to Newton . “I’ve come and gone through that entryway fifty-seven times,” he said . One of the non-uniformed men rose from his chair, hands flat on the table. He was gray-haired with a lined face. “ Newton ?” Newton turned his head to regard the man. A hushed chorus of voices arose, too many and too varied for Ronin to bother isolating any single one . “William?” The plates around Newton’s mouth lifted in a smile. “In all my years away, I somehow disregarded the effect of time. It’s no wonder I didn’t recognize any faces as we entered. Another bit of foolishness on my part .” “I was a boy when you left.” William gestured to the younger man seated beside him. Their resemblance was immediately apparent, despite their age difference. “My son, Will. He has a child of his own .” “That’s Newton?” Rodriguez asked, stoic face turned toward William . “Yes. He’s no threat to us .” “I can assure you, neither myself nor my companion mean harm to anyone here. We came because Miss Brooks was gravely injured, and I lacked the means to tend her .” “Who are you?” Rodriguez’s eyes shifted to Ronin . “I am…was a dustwalker, most recently out of Cheyenne. Ronin .” “You’re built like a soldier. Right down to your hair. Recall any of that time ?” “Images, occasionally. My memory was damaged in the Black Out. I have no specifics .” “Have you retained any of your combat programming ? “I’ve survived fifty-one thousand, six hundred and forty-eight days in the Dust. Ended a lot of individuals that meant me harm. I don’t know what I’ve retained and what I’ve lost, but it doesn’t seem to have much bearing anymore .” “That’s over one hundred and forty years,” someone said. A hush fell over the group . “We…might be able to restore your memory,” William said. “There are several bots here who’ve gone through the procedure with few complications .” It seemed the logical course for Ronin to follow. To learn where he came from, what he’d done before, who he was. To be whole . But what was he missing now? Knowing his place in a dead world wouldn’t do him any good, wouldn’t help his future. He was whole; Lara had given him purpose, given him meaning. His core programming didn’t matter, never truly had. It had served only as a reason to move forward, as motivation to keep him going, and that need had been filled . “My only concern is Lara,” Ronin said . William smiled and nodded, easing into his chair. “ Very well .” The Colonel leaned forward. “As soon as there’s an update — ” “You trust Newton’s word that I’m not here to cause trouble. Let me be with her. That’s all I want .” Rodriguez frowned, brows falling low . “The work Doctor Cooper and her team have to do is very delicate,” William said . “You’ll get in the way.” Rodriguez met Ronin’s optics and held his gaze. “You want her alive, you give them the space they need to work. In the meantime, I want you to tell us everything you know about Warlord. Numbers, armament, defenses. All of it .”
